ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon (HAFNIUM) Exchange ProxyLogon/ProxyShell webshell drop via w3wp.exe
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical HAFNIUM post-exploitation chain on Exchange: IIS worker (w3wp.exe) writing .aspx/.ashx files into OWA/ECP/FrontEnd virtual directories and/or spawning cmd.exe, powershell.exe, csc.exe or net.exe — the exact behavior Microsoft DART attributed to Silk Typhoon during the 2021 Operation Exchange Marauder mass-exploitation of CVE-2021-26855/26857/26858/27065 and the follow-on ProxyShell campaigns.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.process_name) as child from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="w3wp.exe" (Processes.parent_process="*MSExchange*" OR Processes.parent_process="*OwaAppPool*" OR Processes.parent_process="*ECPAppPool*") (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","csc.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_id Processes.process_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=t count as file_writes values(Filesystem.file_path) as wrote_files from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.process_name="w3wp.exe" (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\inetpub\\wwwroot\\aspnet_client\\*","*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\owa\\auth\\*","*\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\ecp\\auth\\*","*\\V15\\FrontEnd\\HttpProxy\\*")) (Filesystem.file_name="*.aspx" OR Filesystem.file_name="*.ashx") by Filesystem.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | where file_writes>0 OR match(child,"powershell|cmd|csc|procdump") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let SuspChildren = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","csc.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","nltest.exe","procdump.exe","rundll32.exe"]);
let ExchangePaths = dynamic([@"\inetpub\wwwroot\aspnet_client\",@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\owa\auth\",@"\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\ecp\auth\",@"\V15\FrontEnd\HttpProxy\"]);
let ChildProcs = DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where InitiatingProcessCommandLine has_any ("MSExchange","OwaAppPool","ECPAppPool","DefaultAppPool")
| where FileName in~ (SuspChildren)
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ReportId;
let WebshellWrites = DeviceFileEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(7d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "w3wp.exe"
| where FolderPath has_any (ExchangePaths)
| where FileName endswith ".aspx" or FileName endswith ".ashx"
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
ChildProcs
| join kind=fullouter (WebshellWrites) on DeviceId
| summarize FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp),
            SuspiciousChildren=make_set(FileName, 32),
            ChildCmdLines=make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 16),
            DroppedWebshells=make_set(strcat(FolderPath, FileName1), 16),
            Hashes=make_set(SHA256, 16)
  by DeviceName, DeviceId
| where array_length(SuspiciousChildren) > 0 or array_length(DroppedWebshells) > 0
ucs.rationale: This is the signature HAFNIUM/Silk Typhoon kill-chain Microsoft and Volexity published verbatim in March 2021: SSRF on Exchange CAS → authenticated arbitrary file write → .aspx web shell (China Chopper / Awesun / Antsword variants) inside OWA/ECP virtual dirs → w3wp.exe spawning cmd/powershell/procdump for AD recon and LSASS access (T1003.001, also in their TTP set). The double condition (w3wp chi

ucs.title: [LLM] Silk Typhoon cloud pivot: service-principal AddKeyCredentials abuse → Graph mail exfiltration
ucs.description: Hunts Silk Typhoon's 2024-2025 cloud tradecraft documented by MSTIC: after compromising on-prem identity/IT-management products (e.g., stolen API keys from PAM and cloud-data providers), the actor adds new credentials/certificates to existing Entra ID service principals or OAuth apps and then uses those SPNs to read mailboxes via Microsoft Graph and exfiltrate to attacker infrastructure.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Change.object) as sp_name values(Change.src) as src_ip values(Change.user) as actor from datamodel=Change where Change.action="updated" Change.object_category="service_principal" (Change.command IN ("Update application – Certificates and secrets management","Add service principal credentials","Update service principal","Add app role assignment to service principal")) by Change.object_id _time | `drop_dm_object_name(Change)` | rename object_id as sp_object_id | join type=inner sp_object_id [| tstats summariesonly=t count as graph_calls values(Authentication.app) as app values(Authentication.src) as auth_src dc(Authentication.user) as targeted_mailboxes from datamodel=Authentication where Authentication.signature="Microsoft Graph" OR Authentication.app="Microsoft Graph" Authentication.action="success" Authentication.authentication_method="clientCredential" by Authentication.user_id | rename Authentication.user_id as sp_object_id] | where graph_calls>50 AND (firstTime - relative_time(now(),"-7d")) > 0 | eval window_minutes=round((lastTime-firstTime)/60,1) | table firstTime lastTime sp_name actor src_ip auth_src graph_calls targeted_mailboxes window_minutes | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let lookback = 14d;
let CredAdds = CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ActionType in ("Update application – Certificates and secrets management","Add service principal credentials.","Update service principal.","Add owner to service principal.","Add app role assignment to service principal.")
| extend Target = tostring(parse_json(tostring(RawEventData.Target))[0].ID)
| extend SPObjectId = tostring(RawEventData.ObjectId)
| project CredAddTime=Timestamp, ActionType, Actor=AccountDisplayName, ActorIP=IPAddress, SPObjectId, Target, RawEventData;
let GraphMailReads = CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where Application in ("Microsoft Graph","Office 365 Exchange Online")
| where ActionType has_any ("MailItemsAccessed","Mail.Read","Mail.ReadBasic","MessagesRead")
| extend AppId = tostring(RawEventData.AppId), CallerSP = tostring(RawEventData.UserId)
| summarize MailboxesTouched=dcount(AccountObjectId), Calls=count(), FirstCall=min(Timestamp), LastCall=max(Timestamp), SrcIPs=make_set(IPAddress, 16) by CallerSP, AppId;
let SignIns = AADServicePrincipalSignInEventsBeta
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where ResourceDisplayName in ("Microsoft Graph","Office 365 Exchange Online")
| summarize SPSignIns=count(), SignInIPs=make_set(IPAddress, 16), Countries=make_set(Country, 16) by ServicePrincipalId;
CredAdds
| join kind=inner (GraphMailReads) on $left.SPObjectId == $right.CallerSP
| join kind=leftouter (SignIns) on $left.SPObjectId == $right.ServicePrincipalId
| where FirstCall between (CredAddTime .. CredAddTime + 7d)
| where MailboxesTouched >= 3 or Calls > 200
| project CredAddTime, Actor, ActorIP, SPObjectId, ActionType, FirstCall, LastCall, MailboxesTouched, Calls, SrcIPs, SignInIPs, Countries
| order by CredAddTime desc
ucs.rationale: MSTIC's March 2025 reporting on Silk Typhoon (formerly HAFNIUM) details exactly this chain: leveraging stolen API/PAM secrets from compromised IT-management vendors to authenticate as legitimate service principals in customer Entra tenants, optionally backdooring those SPs via AddKeyCredentials/AddPassword (T1098.001 — explicitly in the actor's TTP list), then issuing high-volume Microsoft Graph M
